channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: South Africa appoints a special US envoy after its ambassador was expelled
item.description: South African President Cyril Ramaphosa has appointed a former deputy finance minister as a special envoy to the United States after the ambassador was expelled last month.
item.dc:creator: Gerald Imray, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Trump is 'fully fit' to serve as commander in chief, his doctor says after recent physical
item.description: WEST PALM BEACH, Fla. (AP) — Donald Trump’s doctor says the oldest man elected president is “fully fit” to serve as commander in chief as the White House released results Sunday of Trump's recent physical exam.
item.dc:creator: DARLENE SUPERVILLE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Minnesota Senate Minority Leader Johnson is optimistic lawmakers will make session deadline
item.description: State lawmakers have a big hill to climb with just over a month before the Legislative session is over. Despite economic uncertainty, Senate Minority Leader Mark Johnson, R-East Grand Forks, said he’s optimistic they’ll get a budget passed before the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Cathy Wurzer, Lukas Levin, and Gracie Stockton, MPR News

item.title: Video of Trump allegedly 'snubbing' actress Cheryl Hines spreads like wildfire but doesn't show full picture
item.description: President Donald Trump circled back to greet Cheryl Hines despite recent headlines claiming the president snubbed Secretary Kennedy's wife at a UFC fight.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: PETA, animal rights groups praise Trump admin for phasing out 'cruel tests on dogs' and other animals
item.description: PETA and other animal advocacy groups have praised the Trump administration for recent announcements to phase out animal testing at the FDA and EPA.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Republicans fear Trump’s trade war could lead to political wipeout
item.description: Republican lawmakers say there’s a good chance that President Trump's trade war will boomerang on Republicans politically in 2026, as rising prices and shrinking growth could offset other accomplishments by the GOP.
item.dc:creator: Alexander Bolton, NewsNation

item.title: Gretchen Whitmer ribbed online for seeming to hide face in Oval Office
item.description: Democratic Michigan Gov. Gretchen Whitmer temporarily held up a folder to block her face from cameras in an Oval Office meeting with President Donald Trump.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Some top tech leaders have embraced Trump. That's created a political divide in Silicon Valley
item.description: Many of Silicon Valley's most prominent tech leaders have embraced President Donald Trump in his second term.
item.dc:creator: Nicholas Riccardi, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Iran and US envoys hold negotiation over Tehran's nuclear program
item.description: Iran's state-run broadcaster revealed that U.S. Mideast envoy Steve Witkoff and Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi “briefly spoke" together.
item.dc:creator: JON GAMBRELL, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Maher praises ‘gracious and measured’ Trump after White House visit
item.description: The talk show host emphasized that he did not turn “MAGA” during the meeting over dinner at the White House on Mar. 31.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, NewsNation

item.title: Trump administration ends temporary protected status for thousands of Afghans amid deportation push
item.description: The Department of Homeland Security doesn't plan to renew the protected status of thousands of Afghans who fled the country in 2021, when the U.S. withdrew and the Taliban took over.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: K-12 schools face major Trump test on DEI demand
item.description: If the programs are not gone, the administration says, funding could be pulled from the K-12 districts.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, NewsNation

item.title: GOP frustrations with conservatives rise after budget fight
item.description: Frustrations are bubbling up among House Republicans after the conference — by the skin of its teeth — overcame internal disputes to adopt a framework for President Trump’s legislative agenda, a troubling sign for the group as it heads into the next, more difficult, step in achieving the president’s
item.dc:creator: Emily Brooks, NewsNation

item.title: Trump proclaims himself 'in good shape,' but the results of his physical aren't immediately released
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Donald Trump had an annual physical Friday and concluded, “I did well,” praising his own heart, soul and cognitive ability while noting medical reports from White House doctors may not be ready until the weekend. The 78-year-old, who in January became the oldest in U.S.
item.dc:creator: WILL WEISSERT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: California a ‘broken state': Governor hopeful Stephen Cloobeck
item.description: Cloobeck's ideas to turn things around include revamping California’s entertainment tax credit program.
item.dc:creator: Michael Ramsey, NewsNation

item.title: From classifying immigrants as dead to deportation: A guide to actions on Trump immigration policies
item.description: President Donald Trump's immigration agenda is playing out in numerous ways Friday, from hearings in key cases on the government's power to deport people to the start of a registry required for all those who are in the country illegally.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Minnesota Department of Health workers rally against Trump administration cuts
item.description: Members of the Minnesota Association of Professional Employees rallied outside a state office building on the grounds of the State Capitol Thursday to speak out against cuts to public health programs.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: U of M president outlines efforts to combat antisemitism in wake of White House investigation
item.description: University of Minnesota President Rebecca Cunningham sent a letter to faculty, students and staff underscoring her commitment to preventing antisemitism. A key regional Jewish advocacy organization says the school is making the right moves.
item.dc:creator: Mark Zdechlik, MPR News

item.title: Cortez Masto proposes tax relief for working class Americans
item.description: LAS VEGAS (KLAS) -- Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto and other colleagues on Wednesday introduced a new bill in Washington, D.C., aimed at putting more money back in the pockets of working Americans. Democratic Nevada Sen.
item.dc:creator: Madina Ansary, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Real ID rules take effect in May; Minnesota officials say it’s time to apply
item.description: Starting May 7, travelers will need a Real ID or approved alternative to get through airport security. Officials say about 40 percent of Minnesotans have a Real ID. They’re encouraging everyone to get the necessary travel documents ahead of the deadline.
item.dc:creator: Estelle Timar-Wilcox, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrat introduces proposal to ban book bans
item.description: A proposal in the Nevada Legislature would ban school districts and staff from banning books without a court order determining the material is obscene.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Jeffries accuses Trump of tanking the economy to create opportunities for the rich
item.description: House Minority Leader Hakeem Jeffries (D-N.Y.) on Monday accused President Trump of intentionally crashing the economy to benefit the GOP’s wealthy donors and other well-heeled interests.
item.dc:creator: Mike Lillis, WFLA, NBC
